skill.name: c4-architecture-c4-architecture
skill.description: Generate comprehensive C4 architecture documentation for an existing repository/codebase using a bottom-up analysis approach.
skill.summary: Generate comprehensive C4 architecture documentation from code analysis with Context, Container, Component, and Code level diagrams.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. This is a documentation-generation skill that outputs markdown files with C4 architecture diagrams. The skill does not execute external commands, make network requests, or implement cryptographic algorithms. References to shell syntax and URLs are part of documentation examples, not actual execution.

content.value_statement: This skill automatically generates comprehensive C4 architecture documentation by analyzing code structure bottom-up, creating Context, Container, Component, and Code level diagrams with API specifications.
actual_capabilities: Analyzes codebase directories bottom-up to create code-level documentation
Synthesizes code elements into logical component groupings
Maps components to deployment containers with technology stack identification
Creates system context diagrams with personas and user journeys
Generates OpenAPI specifications for all container APIs
Produces Mermaid diagrams for all C4 levels
limitations: Requires Claude Code or Codex with Task tool support
Does not execute or modify code - only reads and documents
Cannot identify runtime behavior or dynamic dependencies
Quality depends on code readability and comments

use_cases.title: New Team Onboarding
use_cases.description: Quickly understand an existing codebase architecture by generating comprehensive C4 documentation.

use_cases.title: Architecture Review
use_cases.description: Document current system architecture for architecture review meetings or technical documentation.

use_cases.title: API Documentation Generation
use_cases.description: Automatically generate OpenAPI specifications from code analysis for all container APIs.

prompt_templates.title: Basic C4 Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the c4-architecture skill to generate C4 architecture documentation for this repository.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick start for documenting any codebase

prompt_templates.title: Full Analysis with Exclusions
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate C4 documentation with target_directory set to ./src, exclude_patterns set to ["node_modules", ".git", "dist"], and include_tests set to true.

prompt_templates.title: Container-Focused View
prompt_templates.prompt: Use c4-architecture skill focusing on container and deployment analysis. Map all components to Docker containers and Kubernetes manifests if present.

prompt_templates.title: API-First Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate C4 documentation with emphasis on API specifications. For each container, create comprehensive OpenAPI 3.1 specs with all endpoints, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements.

output_examples.input: Use c4-architecture skill on a Node.js project
output_examples.output: Creates C4-Documentation/ folder with c4-code-api-routes.md, c4-code-models.md, c4-component.md, c4-container.md, c4-context.md, and apis/ with OpenAPI specs
Generates separate container documentation for each service, relationship diagrams between services, and context diagram showing external dependencies

best_practices: Run on well-commented code for better documentation quality
Review generated OpenAPI specs for accuracy
Use context diagrams for stakeholder communication
anti_patterns: Do not use for runtime behavior analysis
Do not rely on generated docs without human review

faq.question: What is the C4 model?
faq.answer: C4 stands for Context, Container, Component, and Code. It is a hierarchical way to describe software architecture at different levels of detail.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. This skill only reads your code and generates documentation files. It never modifies existing source code.

faq.question: What programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill works with any programming language. It analyzes code structure, functions, and dependencies generically.

faq.question: Where is the output stored?
faq.answer: All documentation is written to a new C4-Documentation/ directory in your repository root.

faq.question: Can I customize what directories are analyzed?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the target_directory and exclude_patterns configuration options to customize the scope.

faq.question: Does it generate API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill creates OpenAPI 3.1 specifications for all container APIs and saves them in the apis/ subdirectory.
